{{Infobox character
| name = Elysian Traveler
| aliases = Elysian Travelers
| appears_in = The Traveler's Gate Trilogy
| titles = Elysian Traveler
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Elysian Traveler Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] are individuals connected to [[Elysia]], known as the [[City of Light]]. Historically, this tenth [[Territory]] represented human virtue and was overseen by these [[Travelers]], who supervised other [[Territories]] and managed [[Incarnations]]. While most [[Travelers]] are trained through compatibility testing, some are born with their abilities via rare bloodlines. For several centuries, the [[Elysian]] line was thought to be extinct following a period of war and eradication by the [[Damascan]] [[Queen]], but a new [[Traveler]] has recently emerged. A prophecy suggests that an [[Elysian]] [[Traveler]] will return to lead the free city of [[Enosh]] against the [[King of Damasca]]. The [[Elysian Incarnation]] is described as a figure of absolute morality, though lacking in conscience or sympathy, possessing total control over the [[City of Light]]. Currently, [[Elysia]] is working to train a new generation of students from various regions to create a force for the benefit of humanity. While some view their [[Territory]] as an abomination or a created entity rather than a natural one, others see it as the pinnacle of human virtue.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Only an [[Elysian Traveler]] can open a Gate in [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The original lineage of the [[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] was eradicated by the first [[Damascan]] [[Queen]]. Although the [[Lirial Travelers]] once served as spies and protectors for them, the last of the [[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] eventually died, faded, or vanished. In later accounts, [[Zakareth]] addresses [[Alin]] as an [[Elysian]] [[Traveler]], a fact that is subsequently confirmed by the [[Incarnation]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] were eradicated by the first [[Damascan]] [[Queen]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Lirial Travelers]] once protected and spied for the [[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] of old.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The last [[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] vanished, faded, or died.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]] addresses [[Alin]] as an [[Elysian Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] identifies [[Alin]] as an [[Elysian Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Regarded as the pinnacle of human virtue, [[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] historically served to supervise all [[Territories]] and manage [[Incarnations]] that breached their world. While it was common for a single family to produce two natural [[Travelers]], candidates underwent months of intensive training in ethics and morality before undergoing compatibility testing. In a significant shift from this past, [[Zakareth]] eventually had a solitary encounter with the one remaining member of this group.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] had a lone encounter with the one remaining [[Elysian Traveler]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] are considered the pinnacle of human virtue.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5">''City of Light'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Potential [[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] receive months of ethics and morality training before compatibility tests.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] used to supervise all other [[Territories]] and handle [[Incarnations]] breaking through into their world.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|It was not uncommon for there to be two natural [[Elysian]] [[Travelers]] from the same family.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“I smell guilt on you, Elysian Traveler,” the Incarnation rumbled.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
